findings.title: Hard-delisting a live market removes its debt from solvency checks and bricks normal resolution flows

best_agent_match.title: Hard-delisting a live market makes its debt disappear from solvency checks while blocking all settlement

findings.title: `_supportMarket` can list an incompatible BToken from another comptroller as valid collateral

best_agent_match.title: Any contract returning `isBToken()` can be listed as collateral even if it belongs to another comptroller

findings.title: Lowering a credit limit below existing debt preserves credit-account immunity and can freeze bad debt in place

best_agent_match.title: Reducing a credit limit does not reconcile existing debt, but still blocks third-party repayment and liquidation

findings.title: Soft-delisting a collateral-cap market clears its controller-side version flag and skips `unregisterCollateral` on exit

best_agent_match.title: Soft-delisting a collateral-cap market wipes its version flag, so users exit without running `unregisterCollateral`

rejected_candidates.title: Listed markets can rewrite their own `version` in the Comptroller without admin approval
rejected_candidates.reason: `updateBTokenVersion` is callable only by the listed BToken itself and only changes the controller-side enter/exit hook selection. In this snapshot that is not a standalone user-triggerable exploit; it depends on a trusted market contract or its upgrade/admin path already acting maliciously.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Missing Liquidity and Collateral Checks
rejected_candidates.reason: Flash loans are intentionally uncollateralized, and the provided code does not include a flash-loan implementation showing an unpaid-loan path. The Comptroller pause hook alone is not evidence of a drain.

rejected_candidates.title: Soft-Delisted Markets Still Allow Critical Operations
rejected_candidates.reason: Soft delisting intentionally preserves unwind flows. `mintAllowed` and `borrowAllowed` require `isMarketListed`, so new supply/borrows are blocked; allowing redeem/repay/liquidation on soft-delisted markets is expected behavior, not a bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Liquidation Incentive Allows Zero Value
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-only parameter choice, not a permissionless flaw. Governance can always make liquidation uneconomic by misconfiguring incentives.

rejected_candidates.title: Unbounded Credit Limits Allow Infinite Borrowing
rejected_candidates.reason: Unsecured credit lines are an explicit privileged feature here. The meaningful bug is the inability to resolve debt after reducing a still-positive limit, which is captured in F-003.

rejected_candidates.title: Market Delisting Doesn't Check Outstanding Borrows
rejected_candidates.reason: Merged into F-001. The reportable issue is specifically the hard-delist path, which both hides live debt from liquidity checks and blocks normal resolution.

rejected_candidates.title: Guardian Can Permanently Pause Markets Without Oversight
rejected_candidates.reason: Guardian/admin pause powers are explicit trusted-role emergency controls. This is governance/operational risk, not a protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: No Price Freshness Validation in Oracle
rejected_candidates.reason: Price freshness is delegated to the oracle implementation. No stale-price exploit is provable from the Comptroller code alone.

rejected_candidates.title: Zero Supply/Borrow Caps Lock Markets Permanently
rejected_candidates.reason: Zero caps are documented admin/guardian configuration semantics, not an unintended code defect.

rejected_candidates.title: No Protection Against Sandwich Attacks in Liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic MEV concern affecting public liquidations, not a protocol-specific vulnerability in the audited code.

rejected_candidates.title: Interest Accrual Can Be Delayed Indefinitely
rejected_candidates.reason: Lazy interest accrual on interaction is standard lending-market behavior and not, by itself, a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on Credit Limit Manager
rejected_candidates.reason: Access control exists: only admin sets `creditLimitManager`, and only admin or that manager can change limits. The candidate describes trusted-role abuse, not missing access control.

rejected_candidates.title: RedeemAllowed Allows Redemption from Delisted Markets Without Liquidity Check
rejected_candidates.reason: If an account is not in the market, that asset is not being used as collateral, so bypassing the liquidity check is intentional. Market cash sufficiency is enforced on the BToken side, not here.
